prs.title: fix(invariants): strip heredoc body before governance path scan
prs.note: Core fix: stripHeredocBody() extracts command header before governance path scan in no-governance-self-modification invariant. 9 tests. escalations.description: PR #1238 from copilot-cli includes stale state file diffs (.agentguard/squads/hq/*, swarm-state.json) because branch was forked before those files were updated on main. Pattern: concurrent agents forking from non-main-HEAD commits accumulate spurious state diffs.
recommendedActions: copilot-cli: rebase PR #1238 on current main before merge
Consider adding branch hygiene guidance: always fork from main HEAD

dogfoodObservations.description: PR #1238 from copilot-cli contains state file diffs from a stale branch base. Three files outside the fix scope appear in the diff: .agentguard/squads/hq/em-report.json, .agentguard/squads/hq/state.json, swarm-state.json. If merged as-is, these would regress HQ state to an older version. AgentGuard governance does not currently validate PR diff scope — a pre-merge invariant checking for unexpected file scope changes would have caught this.
dogfoodObservations.suggestedFix: Add diff-scope validation to the merge workflow. Alternatively, add a governance check: PRs touching only one squad's state files should not modify other squads' state files. This is related to the FileScopeViolation event already in the event schema.

dogfoodObservations.description: Every rtk git * and rtk gh * command in this EM run is classified as unknown action type before the #1202 fix. Confirms the AAB normalization gap is live and affects EM workflows — ~700 unintended denials/day figure remains accurate.
